title: Support modport expression syntax + nested (#2601) (#5581)
body:
Fixes #2601
Fixes #5581

## Summary
Implement modport expression syntax per IEEE 1800-2023 25.5.4, allowing virtual port names
that map to underlying interface signals, e.g., `modport mp(input .a(sig_a), output .b(sig_b))`.
Also supports expressions referencing signals in nested interfaces, e.g.,
`modport mp(output .base_wr(base.wr))`.

### Changes by Feature

#### 1. Basic Modport Expressions (`modport mp(input .name(expr))`) (#2601)
```systemverilog
interface my_if;
  logic sig_a, sig_b;
  modport mp1(input .a(sig_a), output .b(sig_b));
endinterface

module mod1 (my_if.mp1 i);
  assign i.b = i.a;  // virtual port names resolve to sig_a, sig_b
endmodule
```
- **V3LinkDot.cpp**: `resolveModportExpression()` resolves the expression AST (ParseRef, VarRef, Dot) to the target symbol
- **V3LinkDot.cpp**: `visit(AstModportVarRef*)` inserts the virtual port name into the modport symbol table pointing at the underlying var, with a scope alias for cross-scope resolution
- **V3LinkDot.cpp**: `visit(AstVarRef*)` in resolve pass: when an interface ref has a modport, use the modport's symbol table for lookup of virtual ports
- **V3LinkDot.cpp**: `findVirtualPortVarScope()` resolves virtual port names during scope creation when the symbol table lookup returns a modport-scoped VarScope

Supports mixed regular and expression items in the same modport, and multiple expressions with the same direction:
```systemverilog
modport mp3(input sig_e, output .f(sig_f));           // mixed
modport mp4(input .in1(m1), input .in2(m2), output .out(m3));  // multiple
```

#### 2. Nested Interface Paths in Modport Expressions (#5581)
```systemverilog
interface app_reg_if;
  base_reg_if base();
  example_reg_if example();

  modport host(
    output .base_wr(base.wr), input .base_rd(base.rd),
    output .example_wr(example.wr), input .example_rd(example.rd)
  );
endinterface
```
- **V3LinkDot.cpp**: `extractDottedPath()` flattens `AstDot` trees into dotted path strings
- **V3LinkDot.cpp**: `resolveNestedInterfacePath()` iteratively follows interface types through partial `findDotted()` matches when the path crosses interface boundaries (loop with max depth of 64, with progress detection to prevent infinite loops)
- Supports 4-level deep nesting: `modport mp(output .deep4_w(l3.l2.l1.val))`

#### 3. Additional Fixes
- **V3LinkDot.cpp**: `findVirtualPortVarScope()` — defensive null check on `dtypep` before `VN_CAST`
- **V3LinkDot.cpp**: `visit(AstParseRef*)` — when flat lookup in modport fails, set `okSymp` to the modport's `dotSymp` so the "Known scopes under..." diagnostic hint appears (restores pre-routing behavior)
- **V3LinkDot.cpp**: `visit(AstParseRef*)` — handle `AstModportClockingRef` so clocking blocks accessed through modports redirect to the actual clocking block symbol

---

### Tests

#### `t_interface_modport_expr[_noinl]`
Converted from expected-failure to passing test. Exercises:
- Basic expressions with parameterized width: `modport mp1(input .a(sig_a), output .b(sig_b))`
- Two modports on same interface mapping to different signals
- Mixed regular + expression items: `modport mp3(input sig_e, output .f(sig_f))`
- Multiple same-direction expressions: `modport mp4(input .in1(m1), input .in2(m2), output .out(m3))`

#### `t_interface_modport_expr_nested[_noinl]`
New test. Exercises:
- Nested interface modport expressions: `modport host(output .base_wr(base.wr), ...)`
- 3-level deep nesting: `modport mp(output .deep_out(middle.inner.data))`
- 4-level deep nesting: `modport mp(output .deep4_w(l3.l2.l1.val))`
- Data flow verification through virtual ports

#### `t_interface_modport_expr_hier[_noinl]`
New test. Exercises modport expressions passed through hierarchical module instantiation:
- Scenario A: Single-level hierarchy + expression modport
- Scenario B: 2-level deep hierarchy
- Scenario C: Nested interface + expression modport + hierarchy
- Scenario D: Multiple instances verifying no cross-talk

#### `t_interface_modport_expr_bad`
New expected-failure test. Verifies proper error for invalid modport expression targets (non-existent signal).

#### `t_interface_modport_expr_array`
New expected-failure test. Verifies proper `E_UNSUPPORTED` error for array-indexed expressions:
`modport mp(output .ch0_wr(ch[0].wr))`

#### `t_interface_modport_expr_partsel` (updated golden)
Updated expected error output: now only reports part-select errors (not blanket "unsupported modport expression"), and cascading errors are reduced since valid non-part-select expressions in the same modport now resolve successfully.

#### Updated golden files
- `t_interface_generic_modport_bad3.out`, `t_interface_mismodport_bad.out` — added "Known scopes under..." hint lines from improved modport error diagnostics
- `t_interface_modport_expr_array.out`, `t_interface_modport_expr_partsel.out` — same diagnostic hint additions

### Limitations
- Part selects in expressions: `modport mp(input .in(a[7:0]))` — `E_UNSUPPORTED`
- Array-indexed expressions: `modport mp(output .ch(ch[0].wr))` — `E_UNSUPPORTED`
